labels: Progression of bilateral opacities, now more confluent, particularly on the left. suggesting progression of alveolar edema. In the appropriate clinical setting, underlying infectious infiltrate would be difficult to exclude.
report:
FINDINGS: Lines and tubes are grossly unchanged. The NG to cannot be traced through the lower most mediastinum due to underpenetration. The cardiomediastinal silhouette is unchanged. Extensive interstitial and alveolar opacity use in both lungs appear more confluent . Small effusions would be difficult to exclude. No pneumothorax detected.
IMPRESSION: Progression of bilateral opacities, now more confluent, particularly on the left. suggesting progression of alveolar edema. In the appropriate clinical setting, underlying infectious infiltrate would be difficult to exclude.
